23456.headline: Ordrim the Far Seer was named among the gods.
23456.description: Ordrim the Far Seer is a distant creator of stars and knowledge for Night Path of Coreria. The god controls creation, prophecy, miracles, commandments, is marked by a moon bowl, and demands that followers keep night watches from falsehood. Its creation claim says it placed Riveria beneath watchable skies so omens could be recorded; its religious mandate says religion must record signs before action and keep night watches from falsehood. Prophecies come through night charts, falling lights, and a moon bowl appearing without hands, destiny works because destiny arrives through omens carried between places, miracles lean toward omen, and commandments lean toward pilgrimage.

23457.headline: Ilvale the Night Mother was named among the gods.
23457.description: Ilvale the Night Mother is a distant messenger of stars and knowledge for Night Path of Coreria. The god controls miracles, prophecy, is marked by a silver lantern, and demands that followers record omens before obeying them. Its creation claim says it placed Riveria beneath watchable skies so omens could be recorded; its religious mandate says religion must record signs before action and record omens before obeying them. Prophecies come through night charts, falling lights, and a silver lantern appearing without hands, destiny works because destiny arrives through omens carried between places, miracles lean toward omen, and commandments lean toward war.

23458.headline: Quaovar the Lantern Lord was named among the gods.
23458.description: Quaovar the Lantern Lord is a distant judge of stars and knowledge for Night Path of Coreria. The god controls miracles, prophecy, is marked by a falling star, and demands that followers record omens before obeying them. Its creation claim says it placed Riveria beneath watchable skies so omens could be recorded; its religious mandate says religion must record signs before action and record omens before obeying them. Prophecies come through night charts, falling lights, and a falling star appearing without hands, destiny works because destiny is enforced through tests, public duties, and costly proof, miracles lean toward omen, and commandments lean toward law.

23466.headline: Arora Bridge Charge of Night Path of Coreria became doctrine.
23466.description: Arora Bridge Charge of Night Path of Coreria is a doctrine of river pilgrimage under Ordrim the Far Seer teaching that roads and rivers bind distant halls into one people. Its honored virtue is hospitality, and its taboo is turning away a lawful traveler. The religious mandate behind it says religion must record signs before action and keep night watches from falsehood.
